refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is already solution-neutral, specifying what the system must achieve without prescribing how. There is no conjunction or multiple obligations requiring splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to dynamically adjust transmission parameters based on network conditions
Incorporate error detection and correction mechanisms to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication quality monitoring and feedback system to ensure packet loss targets are met
Use redundant communication channels to mitigate packet loss and ensure reliability
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to dynamically adjust transmission parameters based on network conditions

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical approach to maintain communication quality independently by adapting to network conditions, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions can be effectively monitored and parameters adjusted in real-time
Risk that adaptive protocol complexity may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes that the customer-defined packet loss limit is achievable under agreed network conditions
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and feedback mechanisms as separate DPs if system complexity grows, to maintain modularity and independence.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for network and communication management including adaptive protocols

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving data over the network, essential for adaptive communication protocol adjustments.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides data output from the communication module to other system components.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as adaptive communication protocol is core to network management; minimal diffusion as it interfaces mainly through Network Link and Telemetry.
Contains some communication aspects but adaptive protocol logic would diffuse heavily outside vehicle module.
Involved in remote control but adaptive communication protocol is more foundational and would diffuse across other modules.
Safety services use communication but adaptive protocol is not central; would cause high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the adaptive communication protocol DP, as it directly manages network communication and can encapsulate dynamic transmission parameter adjustments with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may require careful interface design to avoid coupling.
Changes in network hardware or protocols might require NGHE Solution to interface with other modules, increasing diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by implementing an adaptive communication protocol that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet the customer-defined packet loss limit, thus fulfilling the teleoperation communication quality requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may increase coupling if interfaces are not carefully designed.
Changes in network hardware or protocols might require NGHE Solution to interface with other modules, potentially increasing diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is highly cohesive with respect to the adaptive communication protocol DP, as it centralizes network and communication management. The module shows a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating minimal diffusion risk and appropriate packaging of the DP within this IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics (≤1% or customer-defined target) under agreed network conditions, which can be observed via the Telemetry interface.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, as this module is responsible for network and communication management including adaptive protocols, ensuring ownership and accountability.

recommended_revisions: Ensure interface definitions between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules are well specified to minimize coupling and diffusion risks.
Establish monitoring and update mechanisms within NGHE Solution to handle changes in network hardware or protocols without excessive diffusion.
Document verification procedures explicitly linking packet loss measurements from Telemetry to acceptance criteria for traceability.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully with clear justification, minimal diffusion risk, high containment, and feasible verification. The adaptive communication protocol DP fully addresses the FR, and the NGHE Solution IM is appropriately allocated and cohesive. Recommended revisions focus on interface specification and verification documentation rather than further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining clarity and containment at this level is preferable to avoid complexity and potential diffusion risks.

revise.why: To ensure interface definitions between NGHE Solution and other modules are well specified to minimize coupling and diffusion risks, and to document verification procedures explicitly for traceability.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol DP fully satisfies the FR under defined network conditions.
The NGHE Solution IM is the appropriate module boundary for implementing the DP.
Monitoring packet loss metrics via Telemetry is sufficient for verification.
open_risks: Potential increased coupling due to dependencies on Vehicle or Teleoperation modules if interfaces are not well designed.
Changes in network hardware or protocols may increase diffusion if not managed within NGHE Solution.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface specifications between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules.
Verification procedure documentation linking packet loss measurements to acceptance criteria.
Monitoring and update mechanisms within NGHE Solution for handling network changes.
